domanda: A sample of DNA contains 32% guanine. Which answer shows the percentage of thymine in the same sample?
spiegazione: If guanine is 32%, cytosine is also 32%. That leaves 36% for adenine plus thymine, so thymine is 18%.

domanda: Which one of the following does not contain amino acids?
spiegazione: Amylose is a polysaccharide made of glucose. The other options either are proteins or contain proteins built from amino acids.

domanda: The direct product of transcription of recombinant DNA can be:
D: the primary structure of a protein
spiegazione: Transcription produces RNA, specifically messenger RNA when a coding region is transcribed.

domanda: Which one of the following is found below the diaphragm in a human?
spiegazione: The liver lies in the abdominal cavity below the diaphragm. The other listed structures are in the thoracic cavity.

domanda: A family pedigree shows that boy C has a genetic condition and no other family member shows the same condition. Which explanation could NOT account for boy C having the condition?
spiegazione: A sex-linked dominant condition would normally appear in at least one parent, unless a very rare new mutation occurred; in this item it is the only explanation excluded.

domanda: The transmission of nerve impulses at a synapse is unidirectional. Which features help maintain this unidirectional transmission?
spiegazione: Neurotransmitter is released presynaptically, and receptors plus gated sodium channels are on the postsynaptic membrane. That structural asymmetry maintains one-way transmission.

domanda: Which structure directly detects a change in blood glucose level and then also responds to the change in humans?
spiegazione: The pancreas both detects blood glucose changes and responds by secreting insulin or glucagon.

domanda: Which row gives the correct sequence of events during part of the normal eukaryotic cell cycle?
spiegazione: During mitosis the chromosomes condense, line up at the equator, spindle fibres shorten, the nuclear envelope reappears and finally cytokinesis occurs. That order corresponds to row 3.

domanda: One set of semilunar valves in the human heart closes when:
A: the oxygen level in blood decreases at the respiring tissues
B: the blood pressure is greater in the left ventricle than in the aorta
C: the blood pressure in the right ventricle is less than in the pulmonary artery
D: another set of semilunar valves opens
E: the blood pressure in the aorta is greater than in the vena cava
spiegazione: Semilunar valves close when arterial pressure becomes greater than ventricular pressure, preventing backflow into the ventricles.

domanda: Which molecules are associated with the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV)?
spiegazione: HIV is an RNA virus with a phospholipid envelope and reverse transcriptase. It does not contain DNA as its genetic material in the virion.

domanda: A circular bacterial plasmid consists of 1000 base pairs. Restriction sites are at 150 bp, 250 bp and 950 bp. After complete restriction, what fragment sizes are produced?
D: 50 bp, 100 bp, 150 bp and 700 bp only
E: 50 bp, 100 bp, 150 bp, 200 bp and 700 bp
spiegazione: The distances between cuts are 100 bp from 150 to 250, 700 bp from 250 to 950 and 200 bp from 950 back around to 150.

domanda: Which of the following could be the outcome of evolution?
spiegazione: Evolution can lead to speciation, changes in allele frequency and increased biodiversity.

domanda: Which molecule is made in both photosynthesis and respiration?
spiegazione: ATP is generated in both processes, although by different mechanisms. Glucose and oxygen are not net products of both.

domanda: A DNA template has the sequence CAC GTT CGC ATA GAC. Which option shows the complementary mRNA strand with one substitution mutation?
spiegazione: The correct complementary mRNA to the original template would use RNA bases. Option C is an RNA sequence consistent with a single substitution relative to the expected transcript.

domanda: Which statement about both asexual and sexual reproduction is correct?
A: Some organisms can carry out both asexual and sexual reproduction
B: Only the gametes and not body cells undergo meiosis in sexual reproduction
C: Mitosis leads to sperm formation in asexual reproduction and sexual reproduction
D: Variation is only required for sexual reproduction and not for asexual reproduction
E: Mutations only occur in sexual reproduction and not in asexual reproduction
spiegazione: Many organisms can reproduce both sexually and asexually depending on life stage or environmental conditions.
